# Open Findings

Use this file as the active backend backlog for unresolved formal review findings.

| ID | Date | Severity | Subsystem | Files | Risk | Next Action | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BE-AUDIT-2026-04-13-01 | 2026-04-13 | high | wechat applyment query | locallife/wechat/ecommerce.go | QueryEcommerceApplymentByOutRequestNo interpolates out_request_no directly into the path and does not enforce the documented required/length contract, so legal business request numbers containing reserved path characters can be misrouted, signed against the wrong URI, or fail upstream with misleading errors. | Escape out_request_no with url.PathEscape, reject empty values, enforce the documented max length, and add focused unit tests for reserved characters and empty input. | resolved |
| BE-AUDIT-2026-04-13-02 | 2026-04-13 | high | wechat applyment query | locallife/wechat/ecommerce.go | The query response is accepted after plain JSON unmarshal with no validation of documented required fields, conditional required objects, or enum sets, so malformed upstream payloads can silently propagate incomplete state, missing verification instructions, or unsupported statuses into downstream applyment state handling. | Add endpoint-specific response validation covering required fields, ACCOUNT_NEED_VERIFY account_validation shape, REJECTED/FROZEN audit_detail shape, and documented applyment_state/sign_state enums, then add malformed-payload tests. | resolved |
| BE-AUDIT-2026-04-13-03 | 2026-04-13 | medium | wechat applyment query | locallife/wechat/ecommerce.go; locallife/wechat/direct_payment.go | This interface relies on generic doRequest error wrapping and emits no endpoint-level structured logs or semantic operator guidance for documented error codes such as RESOURCE_NOT_EXISTS, NO_AUTH, RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS, and RATELIMIT_EXCEEDED, leaving failures dependent on callers for observability and producing non-actionable raw upstream errors at the integration boundary. | Add structured error logging in the query method or a dedicated helper, normalize documented WeChat error codes into actionable guidance, and add tests for known upstream error bodies. | resolved |
| BE-AUDIT-2026-04-14-01 | 2026-04-14 | medium | applyment query downstream propagation | locallife/worker/applyment_support.go; locallife/worker/task_process_payment.go; locallife/worker/applyment_recovery_scheduler.go | Repository callers now recognize upgraded query states such as checking, account_need_verify, to_be_confirmed, frozen, and canceled, but the async follow-up pipeline still only notifies or marks progress for finish, rejected, account_need_verify, to_be_confirmed, and signing-related states. When recovery polling sees FROZEN or CANCELED, the new status is persisted but no follow-up task or notification path runs, so these upgraded statuses are not fully propagated through the project’s background handling path. | Decide whether FROZEN and CANCELED require merchant/operator notification or processed-state bookkeeping; if yes, extend applymentStatusNeedsAsyncFollowUp and ProcessTaskApplymentResult accordingly and add regression tests for both states. | resolved |
| BE-AUDIT-2026-04-22-01 | 2026-04-22 | high | api 5xx error mapping and logging | locallife/api/applyment_bank_catalog.go; locallife/api/complaint.go; locallife/api/subsidy.go; locallife/api/profit_sharing_capability.go; locallife/api/ecommerce_applyment.go; locallife/api/operator_application_admin.go; locallife/api/merchant_cancel_withdraw.go | Multiple handlers still return 5xx responses through `errorResponse(...)` instead of `internalError(...)` or `loggedServerError(...)`. That bypasses the intended structured logging boundary and `ctx.Error(...)` attachment, so request-scoped diagnostics can disappear, while `ResponseEnvelopeMiddleware` may still sanitize the body into a generic 5xx envelope that drops the handler’s intended stable public semantics. In `operator_application_admin.go`, `ctx.JSON(http.StatusBadGateway, errorResponse(err))` also returns an unstable raw error value on a 502 path instead of a stable caller-facing message. | Migrate these handler families to `internalError(ctx, err)` for 500 and `loggedServerError(ctx, err, publicMessage, logMessage)` for 502/503, keep public messages stable and non-sensitive, add focused tests for at least one 500 and one 502 path, then clear the remaining repo grep matches in staged batches. | open |
| BE-AUDIT-2026-05-19-01 | 2026-05-19 | high | subsidy authz follow-up (deferred) | locallife/api/subsidy.go; locallife/api/subsidy_auth.go; locallife/api/subsidy_test.go | The subsidy entrypoints still need object-level authorization hardening before the WeChat platform payment chain is ever re-enabled. The capability is currently disabled, so this is intentionally parked instead of being fixed now; if someone reopens the chain without revisiting the task card, the old body-merchant trust boundary can return. | Reopen [TASK-SUBSIDY-AUTHZ-001](../artifacts/backend-subsidy-authz-task-card-2026-05-19.md) when the platform payment chain is re-enabled, then finish the payment-order-origin authorization fix before any subsidy call is revived. | deferred |
| BE-AUDIT-2026-05-19-02 | 2026-05-19 | high | subsidy idempotency follow-up (deferred) | locallife/api/subsidy.go; locallife/db/query/subsidy_order.sql; locallife/db/migration/000161_create_subsidy_orders.up.sql; locallife/api/subsidy_test.go | The subsidy create/return/cancel flow still needs atomic claim and replay hardening to prevent duplicate external calls or ambiguous retry semantics. The WeChat platform payment chain is currently disabled, so this is intentionally recorded and parked rather than remediated now; the risk returns as soon as the capability is brought back online. | Reopen [TASK-SUBSIDY-IDEMPOTENCY-001](../artifacts/backend-subsidy-idempotency-task-card-2026-05-19.md) when the platform payment chain is re-enabled, then close the claim/terminal-state gap before allowing subsidy traffic again. | deferred |
| BE-AUDIT-2026-05-29-01 | 2026-05-29 | high | Baofu reservation refund/share timing | locallife/db/query/profit_sharing_order.sql; locallife/worker/baofu_payment_recovery_scheduler.go; locallife/logic/baofu_profit_sharing_config.go; locallife/logic/reservation.go; locallife/logic/reservation_dishes.go; locallife/logic/replace_order.go; locallife/db/sqlc/profit_sharing_order_recovery_test.go | Baofu reservation and reservation_addon payment orders became ready for profit sharing while reservations were still in paid, confirmed, or checked_in states, but those same states still allowed cancellation refunds, dish-reduction refunds, or replacement-order refunds. If the five-minute recovery scheduler created and started profit sharing before the refund action, the shared Baofu refund guard correctly blocked later pre-share refunds, turning supported reservation actions into failed or manual-support paths. | Implemented on 2026-05-29: reservation profit sharing now requires merchant `completed` + `completed_at`, completion triggers local profit-sharing bill/enqueue with scheduler fallback, immediate/recovery bill creation reads active `profit_sharing_configs` for the reservation source, and dish-change/replacement-order negative-delta refund creation is folded into guarded DB transactions. No historical-data migration was required because no affected historical records exist. | resolved |
